description: FactorXa_site — IEGR, the Ile-Glu-Gly-Arg P4-P1 recognition tetrapeptide cleaved after Arg by coagulation Factor Xa; the canonical engineered tag-removal/fusion-protein cleavage site.

label: FactorXa_site
note: Coagulation Factor Xa protease recognition site (IEGR; Ile-Glu-Gly-Arg). Factor Xa cleaves the peptide bond on the C-terminal (carboxyl) side of the P1 Arg, i.e. after the terminal R (IEGR|...). Whole-part feature spanning all 4 residues; this is a single atomic functional element and has no functional sub-features.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt P00734 (THRB_HUMAN, Prothrombin, Homo sapiens; reviewed Swiss-Prot), residues 311-314 (1-based, canonical isoform), 100% identity over 4 aa; IEGR is the Factor Xa P4-P1 recognition tetrapeptide immediately preceding the documented cleavage site at Arg-314. Independently fetched via https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/P00734.fasta and confirmed by direct substring alignment (BioPython PairwiseAligner local mode, score=8.0, 4/4 matches).

functional_claims.label: IEGR is a Factor Xa P4-P1 recognition site; the protease cleaves on the C-terminal side of the P1 Arg (after the terminal R)

source.quote: Factor Xa active site substrate specificity with substrate phage display and computational molecular modeling

functional_claims.label: Engineered into a fusion-protein linker, IEGR allows site-specific proteolytic release of the passenger from its tag by Factor Xa

value.founding_demonstration: sequence-specific proteolysis of a hybrid protein to release a native-N-terminus product (beta-globin) produced in E. coli

source.quote: Generation of beta-globin by sequence-specific proteolysis of a hybrid protein produced in Escherichia coli

functional_claims.label: IEGR is the natural P4-P1 sequence preceding a Factor Xa cleavage site in human prothrombin

value.context: Factor Xa cleavage site at Arg; the natural flanking context is DEDSDRAIEGRTATS
value.note: Prothrombin is physiologically activated by Factor Xa; the IEGR/IDGR motifs at its activation cleavage sites are the biological template for the engineered tag

source.quote: Role of sequence and position of the cleavage sites in prothrombin activation

functional_claims.label: Factor Xa cleavage at IEGR is context-dependent: nonspecific secondary cleavage and inhibition by certain P1' residues are documented practical limitations

details: nonspecific cleavage can occur at secondary (e.g. Lys-containing) sites
hydrophobic or otherwise unfavorable residues immediately C-terminal of the site (P1') can inhibit cleavage
steric accessibility of the inserted site affects cleavage efficiency

source.quote: A critical review of the methods for cleavage of fusion proteins with thrombin and factor Xa

functional_claims.label: Host-agnostic sequence element; the cleaving enzyme (Factor Xa) is supplied in vitro, so the site functions independently of the expression host

value.host_dependence: none (cleavage performed in vitro with exogenous Factor Xa)

source.quote: An overview of enzymatic reagents for the removal of affinity tags
